statement: The rewrite should explicitly model start and projection consistency as an idempotent handoff problem, with Temporal as execution truth and Convex as a projected product state rather than pretending the two systems can share an atomic start transaction.
rationale: Temporal start APIs and request IDs help with idempotency but do not create cross-system atomicity, so the safer design is an explicit handoff model.
keyAssumptions: The product can tolerate eventual consistency between Temporal execution truth and Convex UI state as long as projection semantics are explicit
Any DB-first start intent or With-Start path is kept minimal enough not to recreate the current scheduler class of complexity
validationCriteria: No new run or window can be silently owned by both runtimes or by neither
Projection retries cannot corrupt domain state or produce contradictory status rows
critiquePoints: A poorly designed outbox can regress into a new homemade queue
questions: Which flows truly require a Convex row to exist before the workflow starts?
